channel.title: Woodmont Industrial News Feed
channel.description: Daily industrial and CRE updates across key regions

item.title: Gov. Shapiro releases data center development standards - NorthcentralPA.com

item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Google News Data Centers NJ/PA/FL

item.title: CoStar to Acquire Homebuilding Data and Marketplace Platform Zonda for $800M
item.description: CoStar Group, which operates commercial real estate data platform CoStar, is courting the construction business. The company announced Friday an $800 million acquisition agreement with private equity firm MidOcean Partners for Bora and its subsidiary, property and construction data provider Zonda. The deal was paid in cash, with BofA Securities, Houlihan Lokey and Solomon Partners serving as […]
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Edavis

item.title: USPS, DHL partner in a $10 billion ‘last-mile’ delivery agreement
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: AP News Business

item.title: Faropoint acquires 30,000 sq. ft. light industrial building to grow Morris County footprint
item.description: Faropoint has closed on a deal for more than 30,000 square feet of light industrial space in Lincoln Park, adding to its recent flurry of acquisitions in Morris County.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Alaris Aerospace Inks Big Industrial Lease in Palm Beach County
item.description: Alaris Aerospace Systems has signed a 129,000-square-foot lease at Corporate Logistics Center in Jupiter, Fla., landlords Woodmont Industrial Partners and Butters Group announced. The aerospace company, which specializes in the distribution of engine components, is relocating from Pompano Beach. It will use the new Jupiter location for general warehousing, distribution and office operations. The 252,848-square-foot […]
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Julia Echikson

item.title: Data centers: Sherrill eyes plan to manage energy costs, community concerns while promoting AI growth
item.description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill is taking steps to manage the impact of new data center development in New Jersey, announcing a plan to establish guardrails while still allowing the state to benefit from innovations in artificial intelligence.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Blackstone, Brookfield Are Betting On Custom AI — And Proptech Could Pay The Price
item.description: A surge of AI investment from Wall Street titans is pushing commercial real estate toward a future where major firms build their own AI systems, potentially sidelining the proptech platforms that have dominated the past decade. Anthropic and OpenAI’s...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: patrick.sisson@bisnow.com

item.title: Creative Reinvention: Turning Obsolete Assets Into Modern Logistics
item.description: When we talk about creative reinvention in today's industrial market, what's really driving the trend? Location and land scarcity are key drivers, panelists agreed; the group also shared the novel approaches they've taken to redevelopments and how municipalities approach these projects.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Brielle Scott

item.title: Albertsons launches AI-powered produce inspection tool
item.description: The technology supports the grocer's quality inspectors in its distribution centers, Chief Supply Chain Officer Evan Rainwater said.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Jeff Wells

item.title: Data Center Development Raises Farmland Concerns in Pennsylvania - Franklin County Free Press
item.description: Data Center Development Raises Farmland Concerns in Pennsylvania Franklin County Free Press
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Google News Data Centers NJ/PA/FL

item.title: The Definition of ‘Jobs’ in the Data Center Economy Needs to Shift
item.description: The most effective criticism of data center development is also the hardest to dismiss. These projects create fewer permanent jobs than other asset classes, often receive substantial tax incentives, and do not compare favorably to manufacturing when measured by jobs per dollar invested. That criticism lands because it is directionally correct. Trying to position data […]
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Tom Acitelli

item.title: IOS: From Niche Play to Institutional Asset Class
item.description: Industrial Outdoor Storage (IOS), the sub-asset class previously coined the “beautiful ugly duckling,” is spreading its wings within the investment community.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Brielle Scott

item.title: PHOTOS: NAIOP’s I.CON East draws 1,000+ commercial real estate leaders to Jersey City
item.description: New Jersey’s commercial real estate sector was well-represented this week at I.CON East, NAIOP’s annual must-attend conference on the warehouse and logistics sector.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Inside a Mega-scale Mass Timber Logistics Facility
item.description: A powerhouse panel at I.CON East this week in Jersey City, New Jersey, broke down the strategy behind a 2.4 million-square-foot, robotics-ready distribution facility. The panelists shared how collaboration across the capital stack is redefining what’s possible in large-format industrial development.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Brielle Scott

item.title: How Smart Environmental Strategy Protects CRE Value
item.description: Environmental risk isn’t a box to check anymore – it’s a portfolio‑level business threat that can lead to a bad deal if you don’t address it smartly. As climate pressures intensify and regulations tighten, the industrial sector is being pushed to rethink how it assesses, prioritizes and manages exposure across its entire footprint.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Brielle Scott

item.title: Erez Pushes For Board Shake-Up At UMH Properties
item.description: Erez Asset Management is urging fellow shareholders to help remove the lead director of UMH Properties’ board in the interest of increasing the REIT's value. Erez owns about 4% of shares in the manufactured housing owner and operator REIT, which...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: maddy.mccarty@bisnow.com

item.title: Capital Drives the Market: Why Power, Partnerships and Preparation Are Defining the Next Era of Data Center Investment
item.description: The capital chasing data centers today is staggering. But as panelists at NAIOP’s I.CON Data Centers conference this week in New Jersey pointed out, the bigger story is what it takes to deploy that capital successfully.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Brielle Scott

item.title: Newmark’s Chad Lavender: CRE Recovery Gains Ground Across Sectors
item.description: In a recent conversation with NAIOP President and CEO Marc Selvitelli on the Inside CRE Podcast, Chad Lavender, president of capital markets for North America at Newmark, outlined a commercial real estate environment defined by improving liquidity, narrowing bid-ask spreads, and renewed investor confidence across nearly every major asset class.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Brielle Scott
